The research examines the likelihood of the pre-match betting favorite winning and advancing from the quarterfinals of the FIFA World Cup, specifically focusing on matches from 1990 to 2022. ### Research Summary
The research examines the historical frequency of full, across-the-board diesel export bans, focusing on Russia and other major commodity exporters over the past two decades. It identifies four reference-class base rates: (1) Russia has imposed one formal diesel export ban since 2000, which occurred on March 1, 2022; (2) there have been two measures banning exports of refined petroleum products in Russia since 2010; (3) among major commodity exporters, there have been five full export bans on key commodities from 2000 to 2025; and (4) no G7 countries have implemented diesel export bans during the same period. The research notes that there is no historical precedent for Russia transitioning from a partial to a full diesel export ban.

In the current context, as of June 29, 2026, there is no formal measure in place for a full diesel export ban in Russia, although a temporary ban on gasoline and other petroleum products has been extended until July 31, 2026. Reports from April and May 2026 indicate that domestic diesel fuel reserves exceed demand, and while there were discussions about a potential ban due to external factors, no formal action has been taken.

### Research Summary
The research analyzes the likelihood of Ekrem İmamoğlu's release from pre-trial detention in the context of various reference classes related to criminal defendants in Turkey. It provides base rates for different categories, including all criminal defendants in pre-trial detention, those charged with FETÖ/PDY-related offenses, and municipal officials detained on terrorism-related charges. The findings indicate that the overall base rate for release from pre-trial detention in Turkey is approximately 61%, while the rate for defendants arrested on coup-related charges is around 33%, and for municipal officials on terrorism charges, it is only about 3%. The research also notes that there is no available data on the use of judicial control measures or specific charges under Turkish Penal Code Art. 328 related to espionage.

As of June 29, 2026, İmamoğlu remains in pre-trial detention following his indictment for "political espionage," with the next court hearing scheduled for July 6, 2026. Previous requests for his release have been denied by the court, which cited ongoing evidence collection and insufficient judicial supervision measures. The research emphasizes that the situation is fluid, and any new developments before July 9, 2026, could alter the current status of İmamoğlu's detention.
